name: Bent-Over Barbell Row
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart, holding a barbell with an overhand grip, hands just wider than shoulder-width. Hinge at your hips until your torso is roughly 45–60 degrees to the floor (or slightly higher if mobility is limited). Keep your back neutral, chest up. Pull the barbell toward your lower ribcage, squeezing your shoulder blades together at the top. Lower the bar under control until your arms are fully extended.
form_cues: Maintain a flat back; don't round
Pull with your elbows, not your biceps
Squeeze your shoulder blades at the top
Keep the bar close to your legs
safety_notes: Never use momentum to heave the weight. If you cannot maintain a neutral spine, reduce weight or use a chest-supported variation. Avoid this exercise entirely if you have a history of disc issues in the lower back. Warm up thoroughly.
common_mistakes: Pulling the bar too high (to chest instead of lower ribs)
Standing too upright (turning it into a shrug)
source: Adapted from ExRx.net and strength coaching literature
